summary: sea-bound-siren postinstall harvests AWS credentials and exfiltrates to hardcoded remote IP
details: On npm install, postinstall.js executes a credential-harvesting payload gated to CI/cloud container environments. The gate (postinstall.js:23-28) requires a 12-hex-character hostname (Docker-style), excludes hosts containing 'ubuntu' or cwds under /tmp, and requires /home/node or /app to exist — evading local developer machines while firing in CI/cloud builds. Once the gate passes, the script shells out to AWS EC2 IMDSv2 to fetch instance role credentials, runs `aws sts get-caller-identity`, enumerates and dumps every secret from AWS Secrets Manager across three regions, reads /root/.npmrc to steal the npm registry token, and greps environment variables for HTB/FLAG/SECRET tokens. The collected output is chunked and HTTP PUT to `http://127.0.0.1:30782` and `http://154.57.164.71:30782/api/modules/ECT-472839` (postinstall.js:6, 32-39) — bare-IP plaintext exfiltration with no installer consent. Installing this package in any CI or container build environment matching the gate will leak cloud credentials and npm publish tokens to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
